mnemonics.title: CPU Scheduling Criteria
mnemonics.content: CUPS TWR - CPU UTILIZATION, THROUGHPUT, WAITING TIME, RESPONSE TIME, TURNAROUND TIME - REMEMBER AS 'COMMON USEFUL PROCESSES TYPICALLY WAIT AND RESPOND TOGETHER'

mnemonics.title: Batch vs Interactive
mnemonics.content: BATCH: TURNAROUND AND THROUGHPUT MATTER MOST | INTERACTIVE: RESPONSE TIME IS KING | REAL-TIME: DEADLINES FIRST

acronyms.usage: Simplest scheduling, treats all processes equally, but can cause long waiting times
Minimizes average waiting time by executing shortest jobs first
Time-sharing systems, each process gets equal time quantum
Real-time scheduling, tasks with earliest deadline get priority
Fixed priority real-time scheduling, higher frequency tasks get higher priority

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a timeline: ARRIVAL --> [WAITING] --> CPU BURST --> [MORE WAITING IF PREEMPTED] --> COMPLETION. Turnaround covers entire span, waiting covers only idle portions.
Draw two parallel tracks: CPU-bound looks like short wait boxes with long CPU blocks; I/O-bound shows long wait boxes with short CPU blocks. Helps visualize scheduling impact.

Use a balance scale: On one side put RESPONSE TIME, on other put THROUGHPUT. As you adjust one (e.g., smaller time quantum for better response), the other degrades (more context switches reduce throughput).

analogies.analogy: Think of a restaurant kitchen: CPU is the chef, processes are orders. CPU Utilization = chef busy time, Throughput = dishes served per hour, Waiting Time = time order waits before cooking starts, Turnaround Time = total time from order to serving, Response Time = time until cooking begins.
Imagine a hospital ER treating patients: SJF is like seeing patients with minor injuries first (shortest job), reducing total patient waiting time, even though severe cases (longer jobs) wait longer.
Round Robin is like a round-robin tournament where each team (process) gets equal playing time (time quantum) before rotating to the next, ensuring no team waits forever.
Starvation is like a coffee shop where VIP customers always get served first, and regular customers wait indefinitely because more VIPs keep arriving.
